definitions.definition: The legislative body of a country or state that makes laws. In India, the Parliament and the State Assemblies are the legislatures.
definitions.example: The Indian Parliament is the supreme legislature of India.

definitions.definition: A legislature that consists of two houses, such as the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha in India.
definitions.example: The Indian Parliament is a bicameral legislature with 543 members in the Lok Sabha and 245 members in the Rajya Sabha.

definitions.definition: A legislature that consists of one house, such as the State Legislative Assemblies in some Indian states.
definitions.example: The Delhi Legislative Assembly is a unicameral legislature with 70 members.

definitions.definition: One part of a bicameral legislature, such as the Lok Sabha or the Rajya Sabha in India.
definitions.example: The Lok Sabha is one of the two houses of the Indian Parliament.

definitions.definition: A period of time during which the legislature meets and transacts business.
definitions.example: The Indian Parliament has two sessions in a year, the Budget Session and the Monsoon Session.

definitions.definition: The temporary suspension of a meeting or a session of the legislature.
definitions.example: The Lok Sabha was adjourned sine die on the last day of the Budget Session.

definitions.definition: The formal end of a session of the legislature, after which no further business can be transacted in that session.
definitions.example: The President of India prorogued the Lok Sabha on the last day of the Budget Session.

definitions.definition: The formal end of a legislature, after which fresh elections are held to constitute a new legislature.
definitions.example: The State Assembly of Assam was dissolved by the Governor in 2021.

definitions.definition: A body of elected members who assist the State Assembly in the legislative process.
definitions.example: The Vidhan Parishad of Maharashtra is the Legislative Council of the state.

definitions.definition: Proposals or suggestions made by members of the legislature, which are debated and voted upon.
definitions.example: A motion was moved in the Lok Sabha to discuss the economic crisis in the country.

definitions.definition: Statements or declarations made by the legislature on matters of public importance.
definitions.example: The Lok Sabha passed a resolution condemning the violence in a particular state.

definitions.definition: Proposals for laws, which are introduced in the legislature and undergo various stages before being passed into law.
definitions.example: The Goods and Services Tax Bill was introduced in the Lok Sabha in 2017.

definitions.definition: A bill that deals with taxation, expenditure, or other financial matters, which can only be introduced in the Lok Sabha.
definitions.example: The Finance Minister introduced a Money Bill in the Lok Sabha to amend the Income Tax Act.

definitions.definition: Laws or rules made by subordinate authorities, such as the President, the Governor, or the Cabinet, which are authorized by a parent Act.
definitions.example: The President issued a notification under the Essential Commodities Act, which was a delegated legislation.

definitions.definition: A law made by the President or the Governor, which has the force of law until it is approved by the legislature.
definitions.example: The President issued an Ordinance to amend the law on citizenship in 2019.

definitions.definition: A meeting of both houses of a bicameral legislature, which is convened when there is a disagreement between the two houses.
definitions.example: A joint sitting of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha was convened to discuss the Budget in 2019.

definitions.definition: A group of members of the legislature, which is appointed to examine a particular issue or bill.
definitions.example: A committee was formed to examine the GST Bill in the Lok Sabha.

definitions.definition: A committee that is formed to examine a particular bill or issue in detail.
definitions.example: A Select Committee was formed to examine the Bill on the Citizenship Amendment Act.

definitions.definition: A permanent committee that is formed to oversee a particular subject or department.
definitions.example: The Standing Committee on Finance examines the Budget every year.
